questions.scenario: The team is choosing where to run `devpolaris-orders-api`. It is an always-on HTTPS API with a normal release process, managed identity, app settings, and health checks. What is the strongest first filter?

options.text: Choose the Azure service with the most features so the team can grow into it.
Ask what runtime ownership the team wants: managed web app, managed container revision, event function, or full machine.
Choose the service that appears first in the portal.
Use Functions because serverless is the most modern default.

questions.explanation: The article frames compute choice as an ownership decision. The job and operating model matter more than product familiarity.

questions.scenario: Which statements are useful when comparing App Service, Container Apps, Functions, and VMs for one product?

options.text: A product can use more than one compute service when its workloads behave differently.
The choice should include how the team will deploy, inspect health, read logs, and recover.
The newest service should replace the others because fewer legacy names are easier to teach.
A VM is reasonable when the team truly needs OS-level control and accepts the maintenance work.

questions.explanation: The course avoids one-size-fits-all decisions. Each workload earns its runtime by matching behavior and ownership.
